CREATE TABLE xml_test (id INT, data XML);
SELECT * FROM xml_test WHERE id = 1 AND data IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, xml_persistent(file_to_string('doc2.xml')) FROM xml_test WHERE id < 4;
SELECT a.id, b.data FROM xml_test a JOIN xml_test b ON a.id = b.id WHERE a.data IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM xml_test WHERE xml_persistent(file_to_string('doc3.xml')) IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, data FROM xml_test WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM xml_test WHERE data IS NOT NULL) ORDER BY id DESC;
SELECT a.id, b.data FROM xml_test a, xml_test b WHERE a.id = b.id AND a.data IS NOT NULL AND b.data IS NOT NULL;
SELECT data FROM xml_test WHERE data = xml_persistent(file_to_string('doc4.xml')) OR data IS NULL;
SELECT * FROM xml_test WHERE data = xml_persistent(file_to_string('empty.xml')) AND id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM xml_test WHERE data IS NULL);
